title: Concurrency, Cancellation, Timeouts, Retries, and Flake Control · CI/CD Control Room
summary: A delivery pairing session predicts each automation transition before producing the first working bounded execution and concurrency policy for concurrency, cancellation, timeouts, retries, and flake control. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of bounded execution and concurrency policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Build concurrency keys from workflow, branch, pull request, service, or environment identity and choose cancellation from business semantics.
Prevent stale runs and out-of-order artifacts from overwriting newer deployments through revision, environment, and compare-before-mutate checks.
Set bounded job, step, command, network, and shutdown timeouts while terminating child processes and retaining partial evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ci Concurrency Group Cancel Policy
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop older deployment finishes after a newer revision, cancellation skips cleanup, a child process exceeds 2 minutes, and retries erase the first failure. Bound concurrency, staleness, timeouts, idempotency, and flake repair.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ci Concurrency Group Cancel Policy.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for build concurrency keys from workflow, branch, pull request, service, or environment identity and choose cancellation from business semantics. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ci Concurrency Group Cancel Policy, keep Ci Concurrency Group Cancel Policy connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-flake-detection-quarantine-repair-28m5v6". Tailor its execution-control.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair, keep Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-flake-detection-quarantine-repair-28m5v6
  - competency: ci-flake-detection-quarantine-repair
    artifact: execution-control.yaml
    decision: flake detection quarantine repair
    environment: GitHub-hosted ubuntu-24.04 runner on a cold cache
    fault-injection: change the event from pull_request to pull_request_target
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: 28m5v6
    change: |
      concurrency-group: deploy-production-service-a
      cancel-in-progress: tests-only
      revision-guard: compare-before-mutate
      job-timeout-minutes: 20
      command-timeout-seconds: 90
      cleanup: always-idempotent
      retry: classified-transient-max-2
      first-failure: retained
      flake-owner-expiry: team-delivery-7-days
    verify: simulate overlapping revisions cancellation timeout transient failure duplicate side effect and cleanup interruption
    failure: Reject blanket cancellation, commit-order assumptions, timeout as cleanup, all-error retry, or ownerless quarantine.
    repair: Scope concurrency to shared state, reject stale mutation, bound every wait, preserve first failure, and expire quarantine.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: The Ci Flake Detection Quarantine Repair structural check is only one gate. bounded execution and concurrency policy must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
